en.name: Urban Regenerative Water Avant-garde(N)
en.description:
Mediterranean areas face severe drought and water scarcity, becoming major issues and concrete societal challenges. Our living areas are highly vulnerable to the effect of climate change, experiencing more and more extreme events. At the same time, decision-makers plan investments in greening the cities without a long-term strategy on resource availability, impact, and related opportunities, while the transition towards a greener environment requires an integrated vision, harmonised knowledge and stakeholders’ empowerment.
URWAN addresses the critical link between urban regeneration and resources, exploiting nature-based solutions’ role in integrating water management and climate change adaptation. The project involves 9 partners from 7 MED countries, who will jointly implement activities affecting the decision-making process to multiply the impacts of public funds. The partnership capitalises on innovative participatory approaches and existing NBSs while demonstrating the importance of making these solutions multifunctional.
The URWAN Catalogue for final users harmonises knowledge of NBSs using a creative style able to reach all the local players and support the co-design of the proper NBSs to face the challenges. Further, URWAN transforms 3 public buildings into “water producers”, promoting green urban surfaces as a place-based transformative potential by implementing transnational experimental interventions. The project involves decision-makers and stakeholders in 6 cities to co-design multifunctional NBSs in a climate change scenario and deliver Road Maps towards adaptation.
Thanks to the Amplification Strategy and the Nature-based solutions Enabler Pack, the partners demonstrate and transfer the joint solutions and mainstream NBSs. The avant-garde idea is to inspire decision-makers to valorise NBSs as resources for generating urban sustainability, beauty, and social inclusion, i.e., "needs behind functionality", as stated in the New European Bauhaus Initiative
en.expected_achievements:
Increase knowledge between local administration, and urban planners related to Nature-Based solutions (NBS) for water reuse in urban areas to minimize resource use and maximize benefits to better respond to climate change.
Transform buildings and public facilities in water producers merging urban challenges and water management, in a climate change adaptation perspective while promoting the greening of urban surfaces as place-based transformative potential
Improve the capacity of the target cities’ decision makers to engage stakeholders on co-design multifunctional NBSs and tailor investments for more inclusive, beautiful, sustainable and resilient living areas
Multiply and amplify effects of project results, integrating an economic, social, cultural analysis of barriers for facilitating decision makers to mainstream NBS at governance level.

fr.name: Urban Regenerative Water Avant-garde(N)
fr.description:
Les régions méditerranéennes sont confrontées à une grave sécheresse et à une pénurie d'eau. Nos espaces de vie sont très vulnérables aux effets du changement climatique, subissant de plus en plus d'événements extrêmes. D’autre part, les collectivités locales investissent pour verdir les villes sans une stratégie à long terme, alors que la transition écologique nécessite une vision intégrée, des connaissances harmonisées et la responsabilisation des parties prenantes.
URWAN aborde le lien entre la régénération urbaine et les ressources, en exploitant le rôle des Solutions fondées sur la Nature (SfN). Le projet implique 9 partenaires de 7 pays MED, qui mettront en œuvre conjointement des activités affectant le processus décisionnel pour multiplier les impacts des fonds publics. Le partenariat capitalise sur les approches participatives innovantes tout en démontrant l'importance de rendre ces solutions multifonctionnelles.
Le Catalogue URWAN pour les utilisateurs finaux harmonise le savoir-faire en utilisant un style créatif capable d'atteindre tous les acteurs locaux. Pour cela, URWAN transformera 3 bâtiments publics en "producteurs d'eau", promouvant les surfaces urbaines vertes en mettant en œuvre des interventions expérimentales transnationales. Le projet implique des décideurs politiques et des parties prenantes dans 6 villes pour co-concevoir des SfN multifonctionnelles dans un scénario de changement climatique et fournir ainsi des feuilles de route vers l'adaptation.
Grâce à la stratégie d'amplification et l’outil des SfN, les partenaires démontrent et transfèrent les SfN. L'idée avant-gardiste est d'inspirer les décideurs politiques à valoriser les SfN en tant que ressources pour générer la durabilité urbaine, la beauté et l'inclusion sociale, c'est-à-dire des «besoins derrière la fonctionnalité», comme indiqué dans la nouvelle initiative européenne du Bauhaus.

en.name: Promoting Green and Inclusive Mobility hubs for greener MED living areas by leveraging citizens' real needs
en.description:
One of the most crucial challenges in modern Mediterranean urban areas is to reduce traffic congestion and air pollution towards achieving better living environments which are now burdened, given the citizens’ commuting demand, and the increasing seasonal traffic flows. In this context, new, greener, faster and more flexible transportation options with improved connectivity are needed, for the climate-friendly mobility adaptation away from private cars. To effectively promote low-carbon solutions, mobility hubs emerged mainly in Northern/Central Europe, aiming to concentrate public and shared travel modes while providing high-quality users’ experience. However, they are developed in a different context in relation to MED people’s transportation culture and habits, requiring further study and adaptation. 

Are MED citizens ready to adopt these new mobility habits? How can they been persuaded to change their traffic culture to a more green and efficient way? How familiar are the local policymakers with the efficient implementation of this concept after all? 

In order to address these question, the project will develop a joint Strategy for green and inclusive mobility hubs for MED (urban and interurban) areas after considering the vision, the pillars, the particular citizens’ real needs, habits and traffic culture. The overarching goal of the holistic approach adopted is to ensure the involvement of stakeholders, the active inclusion of local communities and citizens, while not neglecting the touristic element as well, for the participatory assessment and promotion of mobility hubs towards climate change mitigation. 

Considering that mobility hubs will be a trend for the next years in Europe, the key outputs of this project will be crucial for mobility hubs’ smooth implementation and consequently for the green transformation of MED areas.
en.expected_achievements:
To set the basis and ensure a strong evidence-based start to the project, by capturing the necessary baseline information, and generating the knowledge which will be useful for the next stages 
To jointly develop a holistic strategic approach by comparing top-down and bottom-up input to address the identified gaps and bottlenecks, as well as by reinforcing citizens’ engagement in the design processes
To promote the uptake of the results inside and outside of the Programme area with the help of the establishment and reinforcement of effective thematic networks on low carbon mobility and environment-friendly habits
en.actual_achievements:
GREENMO project is progressing very well so far, with dedicated and engaged active consortium, deliverable already concluded, as well as communication and dissemination achieved. Everyone is bringing high-energy vibe within the consortium, as well as everyone is genuinely interested in the topic which leads to excellent cooperation and always takes the implementation one step further ahead.

During the reporting period 1, important pre-contracting actions were made, leading to the finalization of key legal and financial issues/documents, such as Subsidy Contract, Partnership Agreement, Steering Committee written rules, Project Guidebook, Quality Plan. 

Lead Partner and all partners were actively involved in all project activities which were officially launched by the kickoff meeting that was successfully held in February 2024 in Athens with the participation of all partners. In addition to that, aiming to engage and purposefully “activate” the partners, LP organized an online pre-kickoff meeting on 23.1.24 to bring closer together the partnership before the official kick off. After that, bilateral meetings between the LP and each PP were realized online on 31.1, 2.2 morning, 2.2 afternoon with two PPs who will co-lead specific activities, 12.2. The aim was to discuss in detail and to jointly draft the outline of each activity with the respective Activity Leader (AL). Indeed, 

Building on the added value of the cooperation achieved through the project, the first deliverable was produced based on the common definition of mobility hubs, the analysis of knowledge gathered, and the input and experience exchanged among partners on the status of their country. The visually appealing handbook "The concept of mobility hubs: Characteristics and Typology of Mobility Hubs in MED Areas" highlights the different types of hubs, the benefits, the stakeholders involved, the peculiarities of med areas and the current state of hubs in the GREENMO partner countries. The final result is a pdf handbook which will be used internally as training material for the participants of the WP2 activities and externally on the GREENMO website and LinkedIn page. It will remain available for the Euro-MED Academy and/or the training sessions within the TCP.

All partners started involving main stakeholders in their territories by introducing them to the project and its main objectives and outputs, which is a task that further emphasis will be placed during the next semesters. In parallel, data to monitor the carbon footprint of the project was collected through the template that the Coordinator created in order to insert them to the calculation tool proposed by the EuroMED Programme.

Regarding communication objectives and activities, the dissemination pack has been finalized in terms of website, poster, roll up, ppt templates, strategy, Linkedin account. Press releases, website articles and posts have been published both on the project’s website/Linkedin and on the partner’s website. Participation in external events and conferences is a task we are working on.

The useful seminars provided for LPs and Communication Officers were attended to get acquainted with the procedures, the requirements, the rules and tips. In parallel, LP’s Communication Manager is part of the EuroMED Communication Officers Network and the Communication Working Group for Green Living Areas. Also, GREENMO representatives participated in the event of GLA Mission to share insights with all mobility projects, while being aware of the cross-fertilization opportunities that the IDP networks/community can provide.
GREENMO project has made significant progress during the first year of implementation, laying the groundwork, as promised, for inclusive and sustainable mobility in the Mediterranean region. The efforts to date, have delivered concrete results, fostered valuable cross-border cooperation and engaged a broad spectrum of stakeholders across different levels of governance and society in all participating countries.

One of the key achievements of GEENMO in the first year was the successful development of a comprehensive training package on the concept of mobility hubs. This training material, created in the form of a well-designed infographic, explains different typologies and characteristics of hubs relevant to MED areas. It has been widely disseminated to policy-makers, public authorities, and educational institutions and will also be available through the Euro-MED Academy, ensuring continued knowledge sharing and capacity building.

In parallel, equally important was the organization of the GREENMO webinar and roundtable, which brought together top-level experts from Mediterranean countries and Northern Europe, including public authorities, research institutions, and private-sector players. The event facilitated a valuable exchange of best practices on governance models for mobility hubs, enhancing awareness of successful implementation strategies. Building on this exchange, the project developed a dedicated insight report on governance structures, offering practical guidance for MED regions aiming to implement or improve such hubs.

The project’s inclusive approach has also been a key target. A series of interviews with local and regional authorities, infrastructure providers, NGOs, research institutions, SMEs, and interest groups allowed GREENMO to capture top-down insights into the planning and operation of mobility hubs. At the same time, the project organized participatory workshops in each partner country, directly involving citizens— of all ages, backgrounds and abilities—to ensure their needs are considered in GREENMO strategy. This dual-input process has been essential in shaping a holistic understanding of the current gaps, expectations, and opportunities.

Another part of the project has been its commitment to sustainability, demonstrated through detailed monitoring of the project’s own carbon footprint. Reports showed that travel—especially air transport—was the major contributor to emissions. Recommendations were developed to help partners reduce their environmental impact, and further actions are planned to promote greener travel choices.

GREENMO has also made solid progress in building interregional networks and strategic partnerships. Through its active engagement in the Interreg Euro-MED Green Living Areas community, participation in European conferences and forums, and visibility through social media and online content, the project has positioned itself as a key player in the transition to more sustainable and inclusive mobility across MED cities.

Despite some minor scheduling shifts—such as the rescheduling of the webinar and the slight delay in completing some stakeholder interviews—the project timeline remains on track. These challenges were addressed through effective coordination and adaptive planning without compromising the quality or scope of the deliverables.

These achievements showcase the added value of transnational cooperation, enabling partners to learn from each other, address shared challenges, and co-develop strategies that are more impactful than isolated efforts. As GREENMO moves into its second year, it is well-positioned to continue this momentum and contribute meaningfully to the creation of greener, more connected, and people-centered urban mobility systems in the MED region.

During the third reporting period, GREENMO partners maintained a strong and coordinated effort across research, data analysis, gaps, communication, and community engagement, advancing the project towards its strategic objectives.

Major progress was made in the frame of Deliverable 2.3.1, the comparative analysis of stakeholders’ and citizens’ views on mobility hubs. Across all GREENMO territories – Greece, Italy, Spain, Cyprus, Malta, and Bosnia & Herzegovina – the consortium put effort on the analysis of the input which was collected based on the extensive and inclusive data collection process within GREENMO, reaching:

- 1,402 public questionnaire responses 
- 130 participants in participatory workshops 
- 60 stakeholder interviews.

These figures are remarkable for a project of this kind and demonstrate the trust built with local communities, the strong commitment of partners, and the project’s dedication to participatory and evidence-based planning. This joint effort provided robust data from both bottom-up (citizens) and top-down (decision-makers) perspectives, now being consolidated by the University of Malta and the University of Cyprus to feed into the project’s overall strategy.

On governance, early in semester 3, the consortium finalized Deliverable 1.2.2 on intergovernmental frameworks affecting the planning and operation of mobility hubs. This work provided insights into institutional barriers and enablers for implementing mobility hubs, as well as specific examples to be useful for interested policymakers. It was further disseminated through conferences and external meetings. 

Communication and amplification activities remained central. The Lead Partner, UniWA, submitted the official project presentation video to the Joint Secretariat (Deliverable D.3.3.1) and complemented it with web and social media dissemination. In parallel, many Linkedin posts and website articles were published covering different aspects of the project progress. Aiming to ensure close alignment with the Green Living Areas Mission (GLA) of Interreg Euro-MED, UniWA together with ALOT advanced the Results Amplification Strategy and they were actively engaged in GLA community events and thematic working group on mobility on international and local levels, strengthening synergies across projects and contributing to mutual learning. Additionally, UniWA’s Communication Officer took part in the Euro-MED Communication Officers Network and in a series of storytelling seminars organized under the GLA initiative (May–June), working on how to effectively translate technical data into understandable narratives for all.

Taking a step further, GREENMO partners represented the initiative in several external events and networks, such as the RuralMED Mobility Forum, SMART LAND conference, THNS forum, UITP Summit, Product Club meeting, GLA national workshops, and the Barcelona community gathering. These opportunities allowed GREENMO to share preliminary results, connect with related initiatives, and position its approach within the broader European mobility and sustainability landscape. 

Consortium coordination was reinforced through regular online meetings and the third transnational project meeting hosted in Malta (8-9 May 2025). Partners presented technical results, co-created future steps, and jointly reviewed the draft agenda of the project’s final event to be held in Mantua in October 2025. The meeting also served to present interim findings from the carbon footprint assessment and stakeholder engagement activities.

The carbon footprint monitoring was carried out by the City of Ljubuški, who collected and processed partner data and issued the report including results and respective recommendations to reduce impact.

Looking ahead, the consortium has started preparing for the publication of project findings to follow, while also securing opportunities for broader policy impact. Notably, GREENMO’s strategy has been selected for presentation at the Institutional Policy Dialogue Days in Brussels (October 2025), providing visibility at EU level.

Added value has come from the interdisciplinary and international cooperation: universities led methodological design and data processing, public authorities engaged their citizens and employees on sustainability, while innovation and consultancy partners steered governance analysis and amplification strategies. This complementary expertise ensured that activities were not only delivered on time and at high quality but also enriched by diverse perspectives, strengthening both local implementation and European-level relevance.

In summary, Semester 3 was marked by successful and timely implementation. This way, the project achieves to keep building momentum towards its final output on strategy, ensuring relevance for policymakers, private actors, and the wider Euro-MED community.

fr.name: Promoting Green and Inclusive Mobility hubs for greener MED living areas by leveraging citizens' real needs
fr.description:
L’un des défis les plus cruciaux des zones urbaines méditerranéennes modernes est de réduire les embouteillages et la pollution de l’air afin d’obtenir de meilleurs environnements de vie, aujourd’hui mis à rude épreuve compte tenu de la demande de déplacement des citoyens et de l’augmentation des flux de circulation saisonniers. Dans ce contexte, de nouvelles options de transport plus écologiques, plus rapides et plus flexibles, avec une connectivité améliorée, sont nécessaires pour une adaptation à une mobilité respectueuse du climat, loin des voitures particulières. Pour promouvoir efficacement les solutions à faibles émissions de carbone, des pôles de mobilité ont émergé principalement en Europe du Nord et centrale, visant à concentrer les modes de déplacement publics et partagés tout en offrant une expérience utilisateur de haute qualité. Cependant, ils sont développés dans un contexte différent en relation avec la culture et les habitudes de transport des personnes MED, nécessitant une étude et une adaptation plus approfondies.

Les citoyens MED sont-ils prêts à adopter ces nouvelles habitudes de mobilité ? Comment peut-on les persuader de changer leur culture de circulation pour une manière plus écologique et plus efficace ? Dans quelle mesure les décideurs politiques locaux sont-ils finalement familiers avec la mise en œuvre efficace de ce concept ?

Afin de répondre à ces questions, le projet développera une stratégie commune pour des pôles de mobilité verts et inclusifs pour les zones MED (urbaines et interurbaines) après avoir examiné la vision, les piliers, les besoins réels des citoyens, les habitudes et la culture de la circulation. L'objectif primordial de l'approche holistique adoptée est d'assurer l'implication des parties prenantes, l'inclusion active des communautés locales et des citoyens, sans pour autant négliger l'élément touristique, pour l'évaluation participative et la promotion des pôles de mobilité vers l'atténuation du changement climatique.

Considérant que les pôles de mobilité seront une tendance pour les prochaines années en Europe, les principaux résultats de ce projet seront cruciaux pour la mise en œuvre fluide des pôles de mobilité et, par conséquent, pour la transformation verte des zones MED.

fr.name: Composer la transition vers les énergies renouvelables locales
fr.description:
Les autorités locales (AL) EuroMED sont de plus en plus engagées dans la transition énergétique mais elles n'ont que peu souvent les capacités adéquates pour mener à bien les processus d'évaluation et de planification lors de la préparation de leurs stratégies locales de transition énergétique durable (en particulier les petites municipalités).
Dans le cadre d'Interreg MED 2014-20, les projets COMPOSE, PRISMI et LOCAL4GREEN ont permis aux AL de rédiger des stratégies locales de transition énergétique durable testées avec succès, grâce à des processus coopératifs qui ont révélé leur potentiel de capitalisation. Malgré cela, les outils de ces trois projets ont été utilisés séparément et le manque d'intégration a été un défaut qui a empêché un transfert ultérieur.
Le projet fournira une boîte à outils optimisée pour soutenir la planification et la mise en œuvre des plans énergétiques locaux, construite sur les résultats des projets COMPOSE, PRISMI et LOCAL4GREEN et déploiera un processus de formation pour l’appliquer dans les nouvelles AL. En outre, le projet soutiendra les principaux acteurs multiplicateurs (autorités régionales, associations de municipalités, agences de l'énergie, etc) afin qu'ils intègrent la nouvelle boîte à outils dans les services de soutien qu'ils fournissent habituellement aux autorités locales. Ce processus de transfert aux parties prenantes multiplicatrices est une caractéristique clé de l'approche du projet et il sera déployé en étroite collaboration avec le projet communautaire thématique (TCP). Des recommandations politiques visant à améliorer les stratégies locales de transition énergétique durable seront rédigées et les décideurs politiques seront interpellés en collaboration avec le TCP et le projet de dialogue institutionnel (IDP).
La capitalisation des projets COMPOSE, PRISMI et LOCAL4GREEN nécessite la coopération transnationale des partenaires pour produire une boîte à outils optimisée et soutenir les nouveaux territoires impliqués.
fr.expected_achievements:
To upscale the results of COMPOSE, LOCAL4GREEN and PRISMI projects (Interreg MED 2014-20) and deliver an optimised holistic approach and an integrated toolkit for local authorities to efficiently plan and deploy their sustainable energy transition.
To transfer the toolkit to key multiplier stakeholders and concretely to local authorities, in order to ensure a wider application of the output for the local energy transition planning processes.
To ensure the impact amplification of the project joint transferrable solutions in collaboration with the TCP and IDP, enabling further exploitation of the project’s tools and their integration in multi-level policy making.

en.name: Composing Local Green Energy Transition
en.description:
The EuroMED Local Authorities (LAs) are increasingly engaged in the energy transition but in general they do not have adequate capacities to carry out proper assessment and planning processes when preparing their local sustainable energy transition strategies (particularly, the small municipalities). Many dispersed efforts have been done to tackle such problems, even by former Interreg MED projects. 
 
In the Interreg MED 2014-20, the projects COMPOSE, PRISMI and LOCAL4GREEN produced relevant outputs for LAs to draft their local sustainable energy transition strategies that were successfully tested, even in cooperative processes that revealed their potential for joint capitalization. Despite this, the PRISMI, LOCAL4GREEN and COMPOSE tools were used separately and the lack of integration was a shortcoming that prevented a further transfer.

The project will deliver an optimized toolbox to support planning and implementation of local energy plans, built on the COMPOSE, PRISMI and LOCAL4GREEN projects’ outputs and will deploy a training process to apply the novel toolbox in new Local Authorities. Furthermore, the project will support key multiplier stakeholders (regional authorities, associations of municipalities, energy agencies, etc.) to integrate the novel toolkit as a tool for the support services they normally provide to local authorities. This transferring process to multiplier stakeholders is a key feature of the project’s approach and it will be deployed in close collaboration with the Thematic Community Project (TCP). Policy recommendations to improve local sustainable energy transition strategies will be drafted and policy makers properly addressed in collaboration with the TCP and the Institutional Dialogue Project (IDP).

The capitalisation of the COMPOSE, PRISMI and LOCAL4GREEN needs the transnational cooperation of the partners in charge of such previous projects to produce the optimized toolbox and support the new involved territories.
en.expected_achievements:
To upscale the results of COMPOSE, LOCAL4GREEN and PRISMI projects (Interreg MED 2014-20) and deliver an optimised holistic approach and an integrated toolkit for local authorities to efficiently plan and deploy their sustainable energy transition.
To transfer the toolkit to key multiplier stakeholders and concretely to local authorities, in order to ensure a wider application of the output for the local energy transition planning processes.
To ensure the impact amplification of the project joint transferrable solutions in collaboration with the TCP and IDP, enabling further exploitation of the project’s tools and their integration in multi-level policy making.

fr.name: Spatial Planning for Offshore Wind Industry Development
fr.description: Le potentiel éolien en mer est considérable dans l'industrie énergétique européenne pour garantir une production d'énergie plus durable et plus sûre, comme l'a rappelé la Commission Européenne dans la stratégie pour les énergies renouvelables en mer COM/741(2020). Le développement des parcs éoliens en Mer Méditerranée est entravée par des contraintes morphologiques, telles que des fonds marins profonds, des contraintes technologiques et réglementaires. Les récentes avancées technologiques conduisent à l'utilisation d'éoliennes offshore flottantes: l'identification de zones marines adaptées à leur installation et compatibles avec les autres activités maritimes est essentielle pour leur développement à un niveau industriel. Le projet vise à soutenir les parties prenantes telles que les autorités publiques, les agences de l'énergie et les PME, actives dans le domaine de l'énergie marine, à prendre des décisions sur les sites maritime appropriés, les technologies éoliennes et les solutions de transfert d'énergie grâce au développement d'un outil WebGIS de planification de l'espace marin transversal. Un base de données maritimes sera consolidé, impliquant les parties prenantes et les décideurs politiques concernés pour une action coordonnée vers la décarbonisation qui tient compte du cadre réglementaire et des stratégies d'aménagement de l'espace marin des pays méditerranéens. Le projet relève tous ces défis et intègre des solutions pour la planification de l'espace maritime dans un outil efficace qui ne se limite pas à l'évaluation des ressources et aux contraintes réglementaires, mais qui évalue et compare les technologies offshore d'un point de vue technico-économique et énergétique. La coopération transnationale est indispensable pour intégrer efficacement les politiques des différents pays en vue du développement de l'industrie éolienne en mer et pour améliorer la coordination entre les parties prenantes, ce qui favorisera l'adoption de nouvelles technologies.
fr.expected_achievements:
Gather MSP data (7 repositories: metocean, various exclusion zones, maritime use, transmission, regulatory) from open access database by the end of WP1, to be assembled and implemented within the WebGIS tool in NETcdf format for the whole Med basin.
Support local and regional public authorities (e.g. municipalities) and industry with methods and WebGIS tool for MSP of offshore wind projects in Med basin. Provide maritime datahub and event organiser tool for transnational cooperation.
Provide methods and comparative assessment strategies of energy transfer and carriers solutions (e.g. AC/DC grid connections, power-to-X) for offshore wind farms which can be adopted by Transmission Service Operators (e.g. Med-TSO, Terna).
Facilitate exploitation of project results involving quadruple Helix and evaluation of the WebGIS tool for MSP purposes (e.g. suitability for feasibility studies). Improve institutional capacity with workshops and public engagement (open days).

en.name: Spatial Planning for Offshore Wind Industry Development
en.description: Offshore wind energy potential is paramount in the European energy industry to guarantee a more sustainable and secure energy production, as highlighted by the European Commission in the offshore renewable energy strategy COM/741(2020). The development of offshore wind farms in the Mediterranean Sea is hindered by morphological constraints, such as deep seabed, technological and regulatory constraints. Recent technological advances are leading to floating offshore wind turbines: the identification of marine areas suitable for their installation and compatible with other maritime activities is essential to improve their diffusion at an industrial level. The project aims to support stakeholders such as public authorities, energy agencies (e.g. FEDARENE, MEDENER) and SMEs, active in the field of marine energy, in the decision-making of suitable maritime locations, offshore wind technologies and energy transfer solutions through the development of a multi-level and transversal marine spatial planning WebGIS tool. A maritime datahub will be consolidated, involving relevant stakeholders and policymakers for effective coordinated action towards decarbonisation which accounts for the regulatory framework and the marine spatial planning strategies of Mediterranean countries (e.g. European MSP Platform). The project faces all these challenges and integrates solutions for maritime spatial planning in an effective tool not limited to resource assessment and regulative constraints but will also assess and compare offshore technologies from techno-economic and energy perspectives. Transnational cooperation is indispensable to efficiently integrate policies between different countries for offshore wind industry development and to improve coordination between stakeholders, which will benefit the uptake of new technologies.
en.expected_achievements:
Gather MSP data (7 repositories: metocean, various exclusion zones, maritime use, transmission, regulatory) from open access database by the end of WP1, to be assembled and implemented within the WebGIS tool in NETcdf format for the whole Med basin.
Support local and regional public authorities (e.g. municipalities) and industry with methods and WebGIS tool for MSP of offshore wind projects in Med basin. Provide maritime datahub and event organiser tool for transnational cooperation.
Provide methods and comparative assessment strategies of energy transfer and carriers solutions (e.g. AC/DC grid connections, power-to-X) for offshore wind farms which can be adopted by Transmission Service Operators (e.g. Med-TSO, Terna).
Facilitate exploitation of project results involving quadruple Helix and evaluation of the WebGIS tool for MSP purposes (e.g. suitability for feasibility studies). Improve institutional capacity with workshops and public engagement (open days).
en.actual_achievements:
The project has made significant strides in advancing offshore wind technology and integration. The four work packages (WP) have collectively enhanced methodologies, developed new tools, and fostered international cooperation.
In WP1 of the project, the focus has been on collecting and consolidating key metocean data to enhance the planning and development of offshore wind farms. Data from previous projects and networks like MAESTRALE, ORECCA, and EMODnet were structured into comprehensive repositories. Significant attention was given to detailed aspects such as bathymetry, visual impacts, and the delineation of Exclusive Economic Zones (EEZs), which are critical for specifying jurisdictional boundaries in the Mediterranean. This allows for clearer logistical planning and regulatory compliance, enhancing the feasibility assessments of potential offshore wind farm sites. The compiled data, crucial for assessing environmental impacts and technology deployment limitations, have been made accessible on the Zenodo portal, ensuring transparency and availability to stakeholders for ongoing and future project needs.
WP2 of the project has focused on enhancing marine spatial planning for offshore wind farms through several strategic initiatives. The team consolidated existing knowledge on marine spatial planning.Significant effort was also placed on state-of-the-art analyses of offshore wind technologies. This involved assessing both fixed and floating offshore wind technologies, selecting configurations for simulations that include multiple turbine sizes and substructure types to evaluate their productivity under different conditions.
Another key aspect of WP2 was the identification of suitable areas for offshore wind farms using the WebGIS tool, informed by stakeholder input and detailed techno-economic assessments. This process was guided by specific criteria like depth restrictions, wind potential, and proximity to marine protected areas and shipping routes, ensuring optimal site selection while minimizing environmental impact.
In WP3 of the project, significant advancements have been made in enhancing the methodologies and technologies for integrating offshore wind energy into the electrical grid. A comprehensive review of power transmission strategies has been conducted, focusing on both AC and DC collector systems and their impacts on grid efficiency and stability. This review has led to valuable insights into optimizing grid connections, addressing challenges such as grid congestion and voltage stability through innovative solutions like FACTS devices and dynamic line rating. Moreover, the project has developed a new economic code for assessing the feasibility of Power-to-X solutions, particularly hydrogen and ammonia production from offshore wind. This code provides detailed economic calculations, including the Levelized Cost of Hydrogen (LCOH), which are essential for evaluating the viability of these technologies for large-scale implementation.
In WP4 of the project, the focus has been on the dissemination, communication, and exploitation of results to maximize project impact and ensure long-term sustainability.Efforts have been made to foster collaboration with other governance projects, enhancing project synergy. This includes the development of an Excel template for tracking governance synergy projects and active participation in forums and face-to-face meetings with other projects like ADRIONWIND and BEYOND, which has enriched the project’s network.Social involvement activities include the development of a communication strategy and enhancement of the project’s web presence, which is crucial for engaging the public and stakeholders. These efforts are supported by active participation in various events and presentations that help to disseminate project results and foster community engagement​
.Together, these efforts not only advance the technical and economic understanding of offshore wind but also promote its adoption and integration into energy systems, highlighting the project's role in pushing the boundaries of renewable energy technology and cooperation.

en.name: Novel Support tools to Mediterranean Governments Exploiting Behavioural Incentives
en.description:
NUDGES - or more precisely, "climate nudges" - are defined as intentional modifications of the people's choice architectures that aim to alter citizen behaviours towards climate-friendly actions while maintaining their earlier alternatives.
The NUDGES Study proposal aims at demonstrating the feasibility of leveraging the territorial diversities and cultural commonalities of Mediterranean countries for the successful integration of "nudging" strategies into the urban climate change mitigation and adaptation policies of local, regional and national public authorities. 
It will do so by delivering six proofs of concept, grounded on the results of 6 experimental pilots, each located in a different country of the programme area, whereby a PP and AP duo secure the active engagement of local public administration alongside citizens and communities.
5 of the 6 experimental pilots will be focused on the potential behavioural influence of collective sensorial experiences using Sight, Hearing, Smell, Taste and Touch; the sixth one will be transversal and focused on new generations.The Living Lab approach will serve as framework to ensure the needed level of realism and depth of engagement in experimentations by Quadruple Helix stakeholders.
In so doing we expect to fill in 3 main gaps in the current scenario: 1) between climate responsible attitudes and behaviours within the MED population, 2) the lack of knowledge at policy level, on the potential of climate nudges as alternative tools to regulatory changes and financial incentives to reach ecosystemic change and community resilience, and 3) the lack of evidence on the intertwining of cultural and behavioural aspects as relevant for the effectiveness, and efficiency, of nudging strategies.
A new and innovative, MED-wide community of interest will be created, paving the way to additional research action in this domain, in the framework of a dedicated Transferability Plan.
en.expected_achievements:
WP1 is lead by PREDA. The first six project months will serve to systematize and communicate externally the substance of the project, which is the identification (by a suitable methodology) of adoptable and replicable approaches to valorising "nudges" as policy tools.
This WP, that is lead by MUSOL, gathers the six pilot activities of NUDGES, each coordinated by a different partner and carried out in a different MED country, at urban level in cooperation with one or more Municipalities or groupings thereof.
This WP, that is lead by e-Zavod, takes stock, evaluates and draws lessons from the results of the six proofs of concept carried out previously. The idea is to evolve from the activity reports to a more structured reflection, clearly identifying needed future steps.
This WP that is lead by ANETEL gathers the mandatory activities fulfilling specific requirements of the Euro-MED 2021-2027 programme.
en.actual_achievements:
During the first period the main focus was to identify a suitable methodology for the "nudges" as well as to have the first discussions with the relevant stakeholders in each pilot area to define the scope and the perimeter of the 6 pilot actions that will start in the next period. More specifically, UdA who is responsible for this activity, has already shared with the partners the steps for the work do be done as well as had online b2b meetings with each partner to discuss and define the interventions and the next steps. Below is the recap from the first discussions on the pilot interventions:
1) e-Zavod: The intervention will include smell walks, scented dining experiences, culinary workshops, and online surveys (Approximately 9-15 people of age range: 10-40 years old). The aim is to promote sustainability, reducing food waste, and encouraging local food consumption. 
2) PREDA: Intervention will be education and awareness programs for children on pro-environmental behaviours (Young generation, children (6-14), and their parents). The aim is to promote waste reduction, recycling and energy saving.
3) MHS: Organizing an artistic contest for billboard designs and outdoor materials to encourage reconsideration of reuse and recycling, specifically fabrics. Approximately 50 participants (both teenagers and adults). Age Range: Teenagers (14-19) and adults (19+). The aim is to inspire pro-environmental behaviours, specifically efficiency behaviour.
4) MUSOL: Promoting healthy lifestyles and reducing environmental pollution. Approximately 50-80 adults and 100 children of the age range: Adults (30-50) and children (3-11). The aim is to reducing car usage, increasing walking and cycling. 
5) AUTH: "Mindfulness and Sustainability" program and appetite framework for 50 people over 18 years old with the 
 aim to promote sustainable and healthy living practices.
6) UdA: Talent Garden, a multi-faceted educational and social project for about 30 kids of age 14-17 with the aim to promote positive emotions and well-being through various community activities and educational programs.

Under the guidance of AUTH the systematic review of literature has started and is still under process. AUTH prepared a guidance of how to do the review, starting from the topic selection (nudges, sustainable behaviours, climate change) and then searching with specific keywords (behavioural economics, nudges, green nudges, sustainable behaviour, climate change) in different databases such as Scopus, PubMed, Science Direct. This resulted a large database of articles that after partners needed to screen based on inclusion and exclusion criteria so as to have the most relevant literature that answers to the question "How effective are Nudges in promoting environmentally sustainable behaviours related to climate change mitigation΄?". The data collection and extraction has been finalised in June and data analysis and synthesis as well as the deliverable writing will be done until September according to the work plan of the project.

Another activity preformed this semester is the creation of the template for the good practise database. The database will be an online tool that will allow users to explore and filter through a multitude of nudges examples based on intervention type, sensory experiences,  behaviour impact and geographic location. This online tool that is now under construction will provide an initial a collection of interesting cases to be explored but also it will be a live tool updated with the pilot activities as well as open to all giving the opportunity to people to join us in shaping sharing their examples of effective nudges towards environmentally responsible behaviour. That could be projects, products, services, and policies that harness the power of nudge theory to drive positive change for our planet. 

For communicating the concept of NUDGES a policy brief has been designed that is introducing nudges concept as well as explaining the rationale behind nudges in public policies. Also, the policy brief describes the way we will work with the pilots and the interventions to be implemented. The policy brief is almost ready, the last design touches are needed for its finalization.

Since stakeholders are an important part the pilot process as well as for communicating the concept of nudges, an internal database has been created with all partners adding relevant contacts. This is also a live document that will be updated during the project life time.

During this period, the project website (https://nudges.interreg-euro-med.eu/) and social media (LinkedIn) have been set up and 6 posts have been published. Also, we have created the project poster as well as template for  the deliverables. 

Also, as a project we have participated to the Green Living Areas event in Turin and we follow all the relavant activities of the GLA Mission.

The second period started with the continuation of the work that was initiated during the first period.  First, under the guidance of AUTH the systematic review of literature was contacted with the contribution from the partners. The D1.1 titled “State of the Art Analysis of Cultural Nudges to Pro-Environmental Behaviour, which has been finalised, offers a comprehensive systematic review of nudging interventions aimed at encouraging behaviors that can help combat climate change, particularly in the Mediterranean region and Europe. The review highlights the most effective nudging strategies, underscores the influence of cultural factors on the success of interventions, and integrates insights from various geographical and cultural contexts throughout the European Union (EU).

In regards to activity 1.2, PREDA (leading partner) with the contribution of all partners has prepared a database tool that contains a comprehensive collection of effective behavioural interventions aimed at promoting environmentally responsible practices. This tool allows users to explore and filter through a multitude of examples based on intervention type (such as culture or nudging interventions), sensory experiences  (hearing, smell, touch, and taste), behavioural impact (awareness, behaviour change, long - term sustainability) and geographical location. A deliverable has been prepared that describes the database and its functionality. The tool can be found in the NUDGES official website, in the tab What we achieve. It consists of an introduction and a video of Nudges and then different tabs of "who we are", "what we do" and the tab "NUDGES with us", which is giving the opportunity to the users to share with us their different nudging experiences and also to give them the opportunity to join the NUDGES community. So far the tool consists of 120 best practises and the aim the online tool to be a living tool to be updated with the submission of new interventions and best practises as well as from the submission of the 6 pilots (when they will be implemented)  activities and results. 

For activity 1.3, UdA as a responsible partner finalized the deliverable 1.3 "NUDGES Methodology Blueprint" that  focuses on outlining a comprehensive approach to applying behavioural nudges and cultural interventions. This blueprint acts as a roadmap for designing and implementing strategies that encourage individuals and communities to adopt more sustainable practices, specifically targeting climate action. A distinctive aspect of this deliverable and of the NUDGES project is the emphasis on integrating cultural interventions, by recognizing that cultural identity and emotional connections are powerful drivers of human behaviour. Based on D1.3, nudges and cultural interventions has been prepared based also on relevant discussions with the relevant stakeholders The 6 pilot interventions have been defined as follow (more details in the deliverable): 

PREDA: uses multi-senses stimuli to engage children and parents in environmental protection through creative workshops.
MUSOL: focuses on auditory cues to reduce carbon footprints by promoting walking over driving.
eZAVOD: emphasizes the sense of smell, connecting citizens to nature and sustainable behaviours through herbal gardening.
UdA: explores tactile experiences to encourage sustainable practices in marginalized communities.
AUTH: highlights the sense of taste, promoting climate-friendly food choices linked to the Mediterranean diet.
MHS: utilizes visual arts, engaging young artists to inspire climate-friendly behaviours through art and satire.

NUDGES policy brief (Activity 1.4) has been finalized and it contains the introduction to nudges and why there is a need to have climate nudges in public policies, a description of the Nudges methodology and how the pilot interventions will work and some recommendations for action. The policy brief has been disseminated to the community of interest that contains more than 164 contacts from different stakeholders from the Mediterranean area. 

During this period, the project website (https://nudges.interreg-euro-med.eu/) and social media have been updated with news. In LinkedIn account we have 101 followers. Special emphasis was given in the 6 pilot interventions with interviews from the partners speaking about their pilot activities. Also, all the deliverables of the project have been uploaded to the website. As well all partners disseminate their activities on their institutional website and social media.

We organized our 2nd meeting in Zagreb (MZ) where we also had the video shooting for the project video. Also we have participated to the Green Living Areas events such as the online working groups and institutional dialogue organized in Brussels.
The 3rd period was focused on the full implementation of the pilot activities. Under the coordination of MUSOL and the guidance of UdA the partners implemented their pilot activities as follow:

PREDA: implemented the pilot programme “Next Generation Citizens – Eco Heroes” in the City of Prijedor. They engaged children aged 6–14 in 4 creative workshops integrating behavioural insights. 1st: Watching the film Wall-E. 2nd: children drew their own eco-hero characters, building personal identity around environmental values. 3rd: introduction to circular economy principles. Children cut, sorted, and ground plastic bottles collected from previous workshops, used Tinkercad to design toys, and 3D-printed toys. 4th: an eco-garden was planted in PREDA;s premises.

MUSOL: implemented the sound inspiration pilot to the city of Madridejos. They created a musical route with sound devices near the school, so that children and parents were motivated to walk this alternative route. They also implemented  a regular nudging  with talks on environmental sustainability in other schools of the area. The activity took place once a week, during the time of dropping off children from school, for a period of 3 months (between February-May 2025). Total participants 44 adults and 77 children.

UdA: explored tactile experiences to encourage sustainable practices in marginalized communities. They implemented their activities in the Rancitelli, Talent Garden between February - May 2025 (every Wednesday Afternoon). The target group was teenagers of 15-17 years old. Total participants: 15. Two workshops were organized, the art and handcraft workshop focusing on hands-on creative activities to support motor and cognitive skills, social skills, emotional awareness and expression. The 2nd workshop was on creativity to climate awareness cognitive shift through tactile engagement. 

AUTH: implemented the pilot activity “Tasting the transformation. They focused on adult18, local restaurant owners, traditional food vendors, and producers in Thessaloniki. They had 45 participants split into 3 groups. The control group visited the partner restaurant and chose meals from the standard menu without any prior education. The cultural intervention group receive education on the Mediterranean Diet, sustainability, and the link between diet and climate change. They visit the same restaurant and choose from the same menu as the control group to assess the influence of prior knowledge. The nudging intervention group receive education on sustainable eating and co-design a new menu with nudging strategies.

eZAVOD  worked with smell. The activity was implemented in May in the Slovenske Konjice Monastery, that has a medicinal herb garden. Participants had a  guided tour of the Žička Carthusian Monastery’s historic herb garden and herbal pharmacy. They explore how the heritage of herbal medicine connects with today’s efforts toward sustainability and mindful living. The cultural intervention was a series of three interactive workshops focused on smell, but also touch, and taste – all rooted in the monastery’s rich herbal tradition (smell walk, tea-tasting experience, introduction to the process of making herbal hydrolates. 

MHS: engaged young talent  by organizing a  contest focused on creating billboard designs to make ctizens to rethink how to reduce garbage and increase recycling. The designers’ contest was launched at the end of May. In total 35 participants participate to the contest. Also, cultural interventions were implemented. A documentary film with a guided tour to the Regional Waste Management System in Gabrovo, and the ”Climate fresk” game with a workshop with ecology experts  was held in mid June (14-15 June). After the cultural interventions, the young designers had three weeks to prepare their designs.  The expert jury made a selection and chose the best design for printing the billboard.

For WP1, we supported the enhancement of the NUDGE4GREEN platform in line with the project’s evolving needs. The platform was technically and structurally improved to ensure complementarity with the main project website.

During this period, the project website and social media have been updated with news. In LinkedIn account we have 141 followers and had  280 total page views and 143 unique visitors. All deliverables have been uploaded to the website and partners disseminate their activities on their institutional website and social media. NUDGES was presented at the EU Green Week 2025 conference in Brussels on 5 June. We participated to the parallel session on  Engage for change: Exploring ways to engage citizens towards circular actions. The concept of NUDGES project was presented as well as the pilot activities followed by a workshop on nudging intervention.

We organized our 3rd meeting in Pescara (17-18 June 2025)  and participated to the GLA events such as the online working groups and the GLA gathering in Barcelona (28-29 May 2025).

fr.name: De nouveaux outils de soutien aux gouvernements méditerranéens exploitant les incitations comportementales
fr.description:
Les "nudges climat" sont définis comme des modifications intentionnelles des architectures de choix des personnes visant à modifier les comportements des citoyens vers des actions respectueuses du climat, tout en maintenant leurs alternatives antérieures.
NUDGES vise à démontrer la faisabilité de s’appuyer sur les diversités territoriales et les points communs culturels des pays méditerranéens pour l'intégration de stratégies de "nudging" dans les politiques urbaines d'atténuation et d’adaptation au changement climatique des autorités publiques locales, régionales et nationales.
Il fournira 6 résultats, fondées sur 6 pilotes expérimentaux, chacun situé dans un pays différent du programme, gérés par un duo PP et AP.
5 des 6 pilotes expérimentaux seront axés sur l'influence comportementale des expériences sensorielles collectives utilisant la vue, l'ouïe, l'odorat, le goût et le toucher ; la sixième sera transversale et centrée sur les nouvelles générations. L'approche Living Lab servira de cadre pour assurer le niveau de réalisme et la profondeur d'engagement nécessaire dans les expérimentations par les parties prenantes de la Quadruple Hélice.
3 lacunes du scénario actuel seront comblées: 1) le gap entre les attitudes et comportements responsables face au climat au sein de la population méditerranéenne, 2) le manque de connaissances au niveau politique, sur le potentiel des nudges climatiques en tant qu'outils alternatifs à la réglementation et aux incitations financières pour atteindre le changement écosystémique et la résilience communautaire, et 3) le manque de preuves sur la pertinence des aspects culturels et comportementaux pour l'efficacité et l'efficience des stratégies de nudge.
Une nouvelle communauté d'intérêt innovante à l'échelle de la région MED sera créée, ouvrant la voie à des actions de recherche supplémentaires dans ce domaine, dans le cadre d'un plan de transfert dédié.
fr.expected_achievements:
Les 6 premiers mois du projet serviront à systématiser et communiquer à l'extérieur la substance du projet: l'identification (par une méthodologie appropriée) d'approches reproductibles pour valoriser les "nudges" en tant qu'outils politiques.
Ce WP rassemble les six activités pilotes de NUDGES, chacune coordonnée par un partenaire différent et réalisée dans un pays MED différent, au niveau urbain en coopération avec une ou plusieurs municipalités ou leurs regroupements.
Ce WP fait le point, évalue et tire les enseignements des résultats des 6 preuves de concept réalisées précédemment. L'idée est d'évoluer des rapports d'activité vers une réflexion plus structurée identifiant clairement les étapes futures nécessaires
Ce WP rassemble les activités obligatoires répondant aux exigences spécifiques du programme Euro-MED 2021-2027.

en.name: Preventing e-waste from polluting MED water by turning waste into a resource.
en.description:
THE PROBLEM
Growing consumption is generating changes in the composition of waste, including 'new' waste streams such as electronic. According to the EC e-waste monitoring 2020, e-waste is growing three times faster than the average waste stream. 

In 2019, EC Decision 2019/2193 established a minimum collection rate of 65 % of all e-waste placed on the market in the preceding 3 years. Unfortunately, in 2019 Eurostat report, from MED only Bulgaria and Croatia achieved 65% target, while Cyprus, Malta, Portugal, Italy, Greece and Slovenia were the top 6 in the bottom of EU, with 20% to 30%. 

E-waste expose a high environmental risk for MED, as informal recycling, and e-waste abandoned in dumping sites, provide hazardous substances seed into groundwater, affecting water sources, aquifers and sea water.

eWaster doesn’t present e-waste as a source of environmental damage, but an opportunity to create INNOVATIVE SUSTAINABLE ECONOMY through the valorisation of this waste. 

OBJECTIVE
EWaster aims to promote local and regional policies for better e-waste management, to reduce e-waste environmental damage in the selected areas, while promoting new innovative eco-business models based on the conversion of the currently lineal electrical and electronic (E&E) sector into a sustainable circular model.

TRANSNATIONAL
The unification of waste management criteria in the transnational MED area is essential, since otherwise it could facilitate the dynamization of the illegal movement of e-waste from areas with stricter regulations towards areas with more permissive regulations. To stop this threat, it is necessary to take into account common joint planning, and coordinated business promotion.

eWaster gather 11 partners and 14 associated in tandems of technical and political bodies, covering 6 EU MS and 1 IPA, which will approve 10 new Action Plans for e-waste management covering the main recommendations of the “Circular Electronics Initiative” from the EU Circular Economy AP.
en.expected_achievements:
To create local and regional Action Plans for e-waste prevention and management, based on the project self-assessment tools and the project “Methodology for designing Strategies for Mediterranean e-waste prevention and management
To demonstrate and test the feasibility and effectiveness of 8 innovative solutions selected from the 8 consortium LAPs, providing validation at small scale prior to applying the solution at full scale and to transfer them to the program Area

WP3 seeks to enlarge the number of institutions up-scaling tested solutions from WP2, and increase the number of institutions using the project tools to create new Action Plans and Strategies for Mediterranean e-waste prevention and management.
en.actual_achievements:
The project began by delivering practical instruction for WP1, detailed with guidelines for data gathering, carbon footprint monitoring, and the communication plan. Data gathering guidelines were contributed to by all partners, who have contact with distinct kinds of stakeholders and seek to gather different types of data.
The first validation workshop was conducted at the Lead Partner's headquarters, where the project was presented, the steering committee defined and its rules of procedure approved, and the Internal Evaluation and Monitoring Unit defined.
Procedures for contracting the external quality evaluator were begun, but not finalised.
For the output of WP1, several institutions and stakeholders have already been identified; it's hoped that these are mapped out and more engaged in project activities by the end of Period 2.
In the second semester, the Environmental Research Institute hosted the eWAsTER consortium in Ljubljana, Slovenia, for the 2nd Validation Workshop. In this event, partners discussed the best approach for data collection, in order to start constructing their regional studies on the status of WEEE management in the corresponding regions and countries; these studies will be integrated into the Local Action Plans.
During the second period, partners collected data from over 400 sources, stemming from surveys and interviews done to the general public, to waste management authorities, NGOs, and public policymakers. At the end of the second period, each partner is close to finalising the regional studies, which will be validated at the 3rd Validation Workshop held in February in Málaga.
Throughout the second semester, ORZ participated in the European Week for Waste Reduction in November/2024, takint the opportunity to disseminate the project among over 100 participants. Similarly, Regione Marche was a speaker at the European Week of Regions and Cities on behalf of eWAsTER. Lastly, PACT, ORZ, and UBBSLA participated in the 2nd ISE Community of Practice, in which eWAsTER connected with other projects with similar resource valorisation goals.
In P3, all partners have delivered the draft LAPs, containing the regional studies for their corresponding territories.
All pilot tests also began structuring their work in VW3, in February. Significant progress has been made, with WEEE BEHAVE making the most progress in working with schools. WEEE REUSE has progress and began implementation, with one partner slightly deviating their implementation by 2 months. WEEE PROCURE is the most legally demanding, and so its full implementation is only expected in P4.

fr.name: Preventing e-waste from polluting MED water by turning waste into a resource.
fr.description:
LE PROBLÈME
L'augmentation de la consommation entraîne des changements dans la composition des déchets, y compris de "nouveaux" flux de déchets tels que les déchets électroniques. Selon le suivi des déchets électroniques 2020 de la CE, ceux-ci augmentent trois fois plus vite que la moyenne des flux de déchets. 

En 2019, la décision 2019/2193 de la CE a établi un taux de collecte minimal de 65 % de tous les déchets électroniques mis sur le marché au cours des trois années précédentes. Malheureusement, selon le rapport 2019 d'Eurostat, seules la Bulgarie et la Croatie ont atteint cet objectif, tandis que Chypre, Malte, le Portugal, l'Italie, la Grèce et la Slovénie étaient les six derniers de l'UE, avec un taux de 20 à 30 %. 

Les déchets électroniques présentent un risque environnemental élevé pour les pays méditerranéens, car le recyclage informel et les déchets abandonnés dans les décharges fournissent des substances dangereuses qui s'infiltrent dans les eaux souterraines, affectant les sources d'eau, les aquifères et l'eau de mer.

eWaster ne présente pas les déchets électroniques comme une source de dommages environnementaux, mais comme une opportunité de créer une ECONOMIE DURABLE INNOVANTE grâce à la valorisation de ces déchets. 

OBJECTIF
EWaster vise à promouvoir des politiques locales et régionales pour une meilleure gestion des déchets électroniques, à réduire les dommages environnementaux causés dans les zones sélectionnées, tout en promouvant de nouveaux modèles innovants d'éco-activité basés sur la conversion du secteur électrique et électronique (E&E), actuellement linéaire, en un modèle circulaire durable.

TRANSNATIONAL
L'unification des critères de gestion des déchets dans l'espace transnational MED est essentielle, car sinon elle pourrait faciliter la dynamisation du transfert illégal des déchets électroniques des zones à réglementation plus stricte vers les zones à réglementation plus permissive. Pour arrêter cette menace, il est nécessaire de prendre en compte une planification commune et une promotion commerciale coordonnée.

eWaster rassemble 11 partenaires et 14 associés dans des tandems d'organes techniques et politiques, couvrant 6 Etats membres de l'UE et 1 IPA, qui approuveront 10 nouveaux plans d'action pour la gestion des déchets électroniques couvrant les principales recommandations de l'"Initiative électronique circulaire" du plan d'action de l'économie circulaire de l'UE.
